title: Rogiera pittieri

The plant is native to Central and South America. Rogiera pittieri is classified within the order Gentianales and class Magnoliopsida. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and evolutionary relationships.

intro: Rogiera pittieri, also known as the 'Rogiera' or 'Yellow Wood', is a plant species native to Central and South America, belonging to the Rubiaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its potential health benefits, including the treatment of fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to support the immune system.
